gauge.name: Chattahoochee River near Fairburn

impacts.statement: Record and widespread inundation flooding occurs in the woodlands and fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92. Cochran Road will be under water up to 9 feet deep. The water level will be hitting the bridge and county officials will have it closed. The USGS gage house under the bridge will be under water with a loss of data transmission. Several farm buildings and the main highway on the Douglas County side of the river will be flooded.

Major flooding continues widespread inundation flooding occurring in the woodlands and fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92. Most of Cochran Road will be under 1 to 8 feet of water. The water level will be hitting the bottom of the bridge and county officials will have it closed. The flood waters will hit the USGS gage house under the bridge with a loss of data transmission. Several farm buildings on the Douglas County side of the river will begin to flood.

Major flooding begins. Widespread inundation flooding will occur in the woodlands and fields along the river upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92. Most of Cochran Road on the left bank upstream from the gage will be under one to six feet of water. The water level will be reaching the bottom of the bridge and county officials will likely have it closed. The flood waters will impact the USGS gage house under the bridge with a possible loss of data transmission.

Significant flooding expands further into the woodlands and fields along the river upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92. Large portions of Cochran Road on the left bank upstream from the gage will be under one to four feet of water.

Significant flooding continues in the woodlands and fields along the river upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92. Portions of Cochran Road on the left bank upstream from the gage will be under one to two feet of water.

Moderate flooding begins. Significant flooding occurs in the woodlands and fields along the river upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92. Low portions of Cochran Road begin to flood on the left bank upstream from the gage.
Minor flooding expands further into the woodlands and fields along the river upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92.
Minor flooding continues in the woodlands and fields in the natural flood plain upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92.
Flood stage is reached. Minor flooding occurs of the woodlands and fields in the natural flood plain upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92.
Bankfull conditions are reached along the river upstream and downstream from the gage on Georgia Highway 154 or 92.

properties.caption: Looking downstream at the Chattahoochee River from the Hwy 92 bridge. Photo credit NWS Atlanta, April 12, 2017, at approximately 0.80 feet stage height.
Looking upstream at the Chattahoochee River from the Hwy 92 bridge. Photo credit NWS Atlanta, April 12, 2017, at approximately 0.80 feet stage height.
The USGS equipment on the downstream side of the Hwy 92 bridge over the Chattahoochee River. Photo credit NWS Atlanta, April 12, 2017, at approximately 0.80 feet stage height.

Photo taken during the September 2009 flooding event, looking downstream at the Chattahoochee River. The highest crest during this event was 30.65 feet (Major Flooding) on September 23, 2009. Photo credit and time unknown.

Photo taken during the September 2009 flooding event, looking downstream at the Chattahoochee River. USGS gage equipment on bridge. The highest crest during this event was 30.65 feet (Major Flooding) on September 23, 2009. Photo credit and time unknown.

impactsLowWaters.impact: Habitat values for trout peak at river flows less than 2,000 cfs.
Habitat values for trout decline as river flow increases from 2,000 to 12,000 cfs.

hydronotes.statement: Automated gauge readings are now available, courtesy of the USGS.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

value.label: Station Datum
value.description: A fixed base elevation at a tide station to which all water level measurements are referred. The datum is unique to each station and is established at a lower elevation than the water is ever expected to reach. It is referenced to the primary bench mark at the station and is held constant regardless of changes to the water level gauge or tide staff. The datum of tabulation is most often at the zero of the first tide staff installed.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
